title: Smoky Earthy Syrian Tomatoes Stew
description: A comforting stew of smoky roasted tomatoes and subtle herbs, delivering an earthy, chewy texture that warms the soul.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Cut the tomatoes in half, remove seeds, and drizzle with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, and sea salt, then place cut side down on the sheet.
Roast the tomatoes for 25 minutes until blistered and slightly caramelized.
While the tomatoes roast, heat garlic-infused oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the roasted tomato halves to the pot and cook for 3 minutes to let the flavors mingle.
Pour in the vegetable broth and simmer the stew until the tomatoes become very soft and mushy, about 20 minutes.
Stir in the fresh lemon juice and adjust salt to taste.
Transfer the stew to a serving bowl and brighten it with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve hot with a side of low-FODMAP bread or a small portion of rice if desired.
Garnish with an extra drizzle of olive oil if you like.
